summary: Completed testing of unified login system with Email+OTP and Google OAuth. Fixed critical bugs in backend where user object was accessed as object instead of dictionary. All 25 backend auth tests pass. Frontend login modal, admin dashboard, mentor dashboard, and candidate dashboard all working correctly.

bugs_fixed.issue: /api/auth/me endpoint was missing is_admin and is_mentor fields
bugs_fixed.fix: Added is_admin and is_mentor to the response

bugs_fixed.issue: verify_admin function used user.is_admin instead of user.get('is_admin')
All mentor verification used user.is_mentor instead of user.get('is_mentor')
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed all occurrences to use .get() method

bugs_fixed.issue: User attribute access used dot notation instead of .get()
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed all user.is_mentor, user.mentor_id, user.id to use .get() method

bugs_fixed.issue: User attribute access used dot notation instead of .get()
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed all user attribute access to use .get() method

bugs_fixed.issue: User attribute access used dot notation instead of .get()
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed all user attribute access to use .get() method

bugs_fixed.issue: User attribute access used dot notation instead of .get()
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed all user attribute access to use .get() method

bugs_fixed.issue: CORS_ORIGINS was set to '*' which disabled credentials
bugs_fixed.fix: Changed to specific frontend URL to enable cookie-based auth

critical_code_review_comments: Root cause: get_current_user() returns a dictionary, but multiple route files were accessing user properties using dot notation (user.is_admin) instead of dictionary access (user.get('is_admin'))
This pattern was inconsistent across the codebase - some files used .get() correctly while others used dot notation
CORS configuration with '*' origin disables credentials, which breaks cookie-based authentication

test_credentials.otp_testing: OTP is logged to backend console when SendGrid is not configured

context_for_next_testing_agent: Unified auth system fully tested. Email+OTP flow works (OTP logged to console). Google OAuth requires Emergent Auth redirect. Mock login endpoints preserved for backward compatibility. All role-based dashboards (admin, mentor, candidate) now accessible after login. IMPORTANT: User object from get_current_user() is a dictionary - always use .get() method for attribute access.

single_login_button.details: Login button in header opens modal with Google and Email options

email_otp_flow.details: POST /api/auth/send-otp sends OTP (logged to console), POST /api/auth/verify-otp creates/logs in user

google_oauth.details: POST /api/auth/google/session endpoint ready, requires Emergent Auth redirect

get_current_user.details: GET /api/auth/me returns user info with role, is_admin, is_mentor fields

admin_dashboard.details: Admin dashboard loads correctly after mock login as admin

mentor_dashboard.details: Mentor dashboard loads correctly after mock login as mentor

candidate_dashboard.details: Candidate dashboard loads correctly after mock login as free user

mocked_apis.email_otp: OTP is logged to backend console (SendGrid not configured)
